profile.name: David Scott Fravor
roles: Commanding Officer, VFA-41 'Black Aces' Strike Fighter Squadron
U.S. Navy Fighter Pilot (F/A-18F Super Hornet)
profile.summary: David Fravor is a retired U.S. Navy Commander and decorated fighter pilot with over 18 years of service. On November 14, 2004, while commanding VFA-41 during a routine training exercise off the coast of southern California, he was vectored by USS Princeton to investigate an anomalous radar contact. What he and three other aviators observed — a 40-foot white tic-tac-shaped object with no wings, no exhaust, and instantaneous directional changes — became the most credible documented UAP encounter in modern history. The encounter was corroborated by multiple radar systems, two F/A-18 crews, and later confirmed by the Pentagon as authentic. Fravor testified before the House Oversight Committee on July 26, 2023, making him the first military aviator to testify under oath about a direct UAP encounter in a modern congressional hearing.
education: Naval Aviator flight training, Naval Air Station Meridian
early_career: Graduated U.S. Naval Academy 1987; commissioned as Ensign
Completed flight training; designated Naval Aviator

key_events.event: Graduates Naval Academy; begins Navy career
Multiple combat deployments; becomes CO of VFA-41 Black Aces

The Tic Tac encounter — vectored by USS Princeton CIC to investigate radar anomaly; observes white 40-ft oblong object with no propulsion signature performing impossible maneuvers 80 miles southwest of San Diego; object detected intermittently on radar for weeks prior
A second F/A-18 crew (Lt. Chad Underwood) captures the FLIR1 footage of a different contact from the same exercise area — this becomes the famous 'Tic Tac video'
Retires from U.S. Navy after 18+ years; moves to private sector (later runs a catering business in Massachusetts)
New York Times publishes 'Glowing Auras and Black Money' — AATIP confirmed, Nimitz encounter reported publicly for the first time
Pentagon officially acknowledges FLIR1/Nimitz video as authentic Navy gun-camera footage

Testifies under oath before House Oversight Subcommittee on National Security alongside David Grusch and Ryan Graves — first time a military pilot testified under oath about a direct UAP encounter in a modern hearing
Congress releases written transcripts of closed-door classified briefings referencing the Nimitz encounter in program context

encounter.location: Pacific Ocean, approximately 100 miles southwest of San Diego, California
encounter.detection_platform: USS Princeton (CG-59) — AN/SPY-1B Aegis Combat System radar
encounter.prior_tracking: USS Princeton radar tracked unidentified objects descending from 80,000 ft to near sea-level and returning multiple times over approximately 2 weeks before the intercept
encounter.fravor_aircraft: F/A-18F Super Hornet, VFA-41 Black Aces
encounter.wingman: Lt. Commander Jim Slaight (with Lt. Cmdr. Alex Dietrich in second aircraft)
encounter.object_description: Approximately 40 feet long, white, oblong/'tic-tac' shaped. No wings. No tail. No visible propulsion or exhaust. No infrared signature consistent with jet propulsion. Smooth surface.
observed_behavior: Hovering at approximately 50 feet above ocean surface over a disturbed patch of white water
Mirroring Fravor's aircraft movement when he attempted to cut off the descent
Instantaneous acceleration away when Fravor rolled inverted and descended toward it
Accelerated to beyond visual range in under 2 seconds — estimated cover of 60 miles
Reappeared on USS Princeton radar at CAP point where Fravor's aircraft was originally stationed — 60 miles away — within seconds
encounter.flir_footage: The famous FLIR1 'Tic Tac video' was captured later that day by Lt. Chad Underwood in a different aircraft, on a different contact in the same area. Fravor was not in the aircraft that recorded the video.
witnesses: Lt. Commander Alex Dietrich (second aircraft pilot)

claims.claim: On November 14, 2004, he observed a 40-foot white oblong object performing flight maneuvers impossible by known human technology, including hovering, instantaneous acceleration, and apparent awareness of his aircraft.
claims.notes: Three other aviators corroborate the encounter. USS Princeton radar independently tracked anomalous objects for weeks. Pentagon confirmed the FLIR video authentic. The object's specific capabilities (instantaneous acceleration, trans-medium) remain unexplained by official investigations.

claims.claim: The object displayed flight characteristics — no visible propulsion, no sonic boom, no exhaust plume, extreme acceleration — that are inconsistent with any known U.S., Russian, or Chinese aerospace technology as of 2004 or today.
claims.notes: AARO's 2024 report does not explain the Nimitz encounter. Consistent with the Five Observables framework Elizondo later formulated.

claims.claim: The object appeared to actively respond to and mirror Fravor's aircraft maneuvers, suggesting either controlled operation or autonomous response capability far exceeding known drone technology.
claims.notes: Fravor's consistent testimony across dozens of interviews. Corroborated by his wingman.

claims.claim: USS Princeton's Aegis radar system tracked anomalous objects descending from 80,000 ft to near sea-level and returning over a period of approximately 2 weeks prior to the visual intercept.
claims.notes: USS Princeton CIC operators corroborate. The radar system is among the most advanced in service. Some radar anomalies attributed to potential software malfunction by AARO.

claims.claim: The incident was formally reported up the chain of command and the data was collected. Senior personnel were aware. It was not treated as significant until years later.
claims.notes: Fravor has stated this in interviews. The footage's subsequent classification and later release via AATIP/Elizondo suggests formal program interest.

claims.claim: His July 2023 congressional testimony was accurate and consistent with his account as given in every interview since 2017.
claims.notes: Cross-referencing his 2017-2023 public accounts with his sworn testimony shows remarkable consistency. No material contradictions have emerged.

supporting: Decorated Navy Commander with 6,000+ flight hours and 2,000+ carrier landings — highly trained observer
Three additional aviators corroborate the visual encounter in separate accounts
USS Princeton radar independently tracked anomalous objects for weeks
Pentagon confirmed the FLIR1 Nimitz video as authentic Navy footage in April 2019
Consistency of testimony across 6+ years of public interviews with no material contradictions
No financial motive — testified without payment, runs catering business
Congressional testimony under oath in 2023 with no recantation
contradicting: AARO (2024) suggested some USS Princeton radar anomalies may have been attributable to radar mode switching software — but did not explain the visual encounter

associated_people.name: David Grusch
associated_people.role: Former NRO intelligence officer; co-founder, Sol Foundation
associated_people.relationship: Testified alongside Fravor at the July 26, 2023 House Oversight hearing; Grusch's program-level claims provide broader context for the government awareness Fravor described

associated_people.name: Ryan Graves
associated_people.role: Former U.S. Navy F/A-18 pilot; Executive Director, Americans for Safe Aerospace
associated_people.relationship: Testified alongside Fravor at the July 26, 2023 House Oversight hearing; independently corroborates UAP encounter patterns and pilot stigma

associated_people.name: Luis Elizondo
associated_people.role: Former Director, AATIP; co-founder, To The Stars Academy
associated_people.relationship: Released the FLIR1 Tic Tac video through AATIP and To The Stars Academy in 2017; the Nimitz encounter was the primary case AATIP investigated

associated_people.name: Jim Slaight
associated_people.role: Lt. Commander, U.S. Navy (Ret.); Fravor's wingman on November 14, 2004
associated_people.relationship: Flew as Fravor's wingman during the Tic Tac encounter; has corroborated the encounter in interviews and congressional record

associated_people.name: Alex Dietrich
associated_people.role: Lt. Commander, U.S. Navy (Ret.); second aircraft pilot
associated_people.relationship: Piloted the second F/A-18 during the Tic Tac encounter; provided corroborating account in 60 Minutes interview (2021) — the first time she went on record publicly

disclosures.title: New York Times — Nimitz encounter first public report
disclosures.outlet: The New York Times / AATIP reporting
disclosures.notes: Fravor's encounter confirmed through AATIP reporting; first mainstream public account of the Tic Tac incident

disclosures.title: Pentagon confirmation of authentic FLIR1 footage
disclosures.notes: Pentagon officially acknowledged the FLIR1 Nimitz video as authentic Navy gun-camera footage of unidentified aerial phenomena

disclosures.title: House Oversight Subcommittee on National Security — UAP Hearing
disclosures.notes: Fravor testified under oath alongside Grusch and Graves; first military aviator to testify under oath about a direct UAP encounter in a modern congressional hearing

disclosures.title: Multiple media appearances
disclosures.outlet: 60 Minutes, Joe Rogan Experience, NewsNation, CNN, Fox News, and others
disclosures.notes: Sustained public media campaign since 2017; no material contradictions have emerged across years of interviews

career_connections.relationship: Dietrich piloted the second F/A-18 during the Tic Tac encounter; first public corroborating account on 60 Minutes in 2021
The Nimitz encounter was the central AATIP case; FLIR1 video became the most studied evidence from the program
Elizondo released the FLIR1 Tic Tac video through AATIP and TTSA in 2017; Fravor encounter was the primary AATIP case
Co-testified at July 26, 2023 House hearing; Fravor direct encounter provided experiential grounding for Grusch program disclosures
Co-testified at July 2023 hearing; both military aviator witnesses whose combined accounts anchored the congressional session
